2605.26277.title: VesselSim: learning 3D blood vessel segmentation without expert annotations
2605.26277.paper_summary: Blood vessel segmentation is a core task in medical image analysis for the care of vascular diseases and surgical planning, yet the challenges of providing expert vascular annotations pose a major obstacle for the progress of related deep learning techniques. To address this, we propose VesselSim, a two-stage framework for universal 3D blood vessel segmentation that eliminates the need for real annotated data during training. First, we introduce a stochastic, geometry-driven vascular simulation framework that models recursive branching, curvature-controlled growth, and collision-aware topology, followed by domain-randomized intensity synthesis to generate 16,500 anatomically plausible 3D angiographic volumes. Second, a 3D U-Net is trained solely on this synthetic data. To bridge the domain gap from synthetic to real images at inference time, we introduce a test-time adaptation strategy via a self-supervised mask reconstruction decoder, enabling adaptation to unseen clinical scans without prior domain knowledge. We evaluate VesselSim in a zero-shot setting on multiple real-world datasets spanning MR and CT across several anatomical regions, including the brain and kidneys. Despite being trained exclusively on synthetic data, VesselSim achieves performance competitive with state-of-the-art vascular segmentation foundation models. These findings suggest that learning vessel geometry from synthetic tubular structures is effective for robust cross-domain generalization, substantially reducing the reliance on acquired medical imaging data and more importantly, expert annotations.
2605.26277.paper_summary_zh: 血管分割是醫學影像分析中一項核心任務，對於血管疾病的護理和手術規劃至關重要，然而提供專家血管註釋的挑戰對相關深度學習技術的進展構成了主要障礙。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了VesselSim，一個兩階段的通用3D血管分割框架，消除了訓練期間對真實註釋數據的需求。首先，我們引入了一個隨機的、以幾何為驅動的血管模擬框架，該框架模擬了遞歸分支、曲率控制的生長和碰撞感知的拓撲，隨後進行領域隨機化強度合成，以生成16,500個解剖上合理的3D血管造影體積。其次，僅在這些合成數據上訓練3D U-Net。為了在推理時縮小合成圖像與真實圖像之間的領域差距，我們引入了一種測試時適應策略，通過自我監督的掩碼重建解碼器，使其能夠在沒有先前領域知識的情況下適應未見的臨床掃描。我們在多個真實世界數據集上對VesselSim進行了零樣本設置的評估，這些數據集涵蓋了多個解剖區域，包括大腦和腎臟，並跨越MR和CT。儘管僅在合成數據上進行訓練，VesselSim的性能仍然與最先進的血管分割基礎模型相媲美。這些發現表明，從合成管狀結構中學習血管幾何對於穩健的跨領域泛化是有效的，顯著減少了對獲取的醫學影像數據的依賴，更重要的是，減少了對專家註釋的需求。
2605.26277.author: Erin Rainville et.al.
2605.26277.authors: Erin Rainville, Melissa Ananian, Tristan Mirolla, Hassan Rivaz, Yiming Xiao
